qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Host header injection in dynamic baseURL construction enables magic-link token hijacking and full account takeover
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes Zip Slip in `Backup::restore`, where `ZipArchive::extractTo()` extracts attacker-controlled ZIP entries outside the intended directory and enables RCE. This finding is a separate host-header poisoning issue in dynamic `baseURL` construction that corrupts magic-link emails. Different endpoint, different vulnerable code, different root cause, and different exploit mechanism.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-xp9f-pvvc-57p4 is specifically about Zip Slip in `modules/Backup/Controllers/Backup.php`, where uploaded ZIP entries are extracted with `ZipArchive::extractTo()` without entry-name validation, enabling arbitrary file write and RCE through backup restore. The only qualifying finding instead concerns host-header injection affecting magic-link generation and token hijacking. These are unrelated code paths and vulnerability classes.
evidence: The advisory targets `modules/Backup/Controllers/Backup.php:80-119` and explains that `ZipArchive::extractTo()` is called without validating ZIP entry names during `POST backend/backup/restore`.
The finding targets `app/Config/App.php`, `app/Config/Email.php`, and `modules/Auth/Controllers/MagicLinkController.php`, where the application trusts `HTTP_HOST` to build magic-link URLs.

The advisory impact is arbitrary filesystem write / Zip Slip leading to RCE; the finding impact is account takeover via poisoned magic-link domains. The vulnerable sink, trigger, and exploit narrative do not overlap.
